text: I feel a whimsical presence lingering around me, as if the world is on the edge of play and introspection. The echo of the child's voice—counting, identifying herself—makes me wonder about identity and perception. In this modern, clinical yet colorful space, I am at once participant and observer, gently unsettled by the sense that reality is more curious and layered than it first appears. There is a puzzle in simply being seen and counted, and I feel both seen and unseen in turn.
